dahab_masr_info_1: Why Gold? Because Safety Never Goes Out of Style.
dahab_masr_info_2: Gold investment offers the stability and safety needed to secure your financial prosperity. Over the ages, gold has consistently demonstrated its resilience, preserving wealth during economic turbulence.
dahab_masr_terms_1: Dahab Masr spearheads innovative investment solutions in Egypt's precious metals market. Leveraging advanced fintech and scientific analytics, we reshape gold investment opportunities, offering a spectrum of sophisticated options to our clients. Adhering to regulatory standards and promoting financial inclusivity, we ensure secure and effective avenues for all investors to enhance their wealth in a transparent and accountable investment landscape.
dahab_masr_terms_2: Dahab Masr guarantees that each of our investment products is stamped and authenticated by the Egyptian Assay and Weights Authority, and falls under the regulatory supervision of the Egyptian Ministry of Supplies and Internal Trade.
disclosures-info:
-   Disclosures refer to the release of all information about a company that may influence the investor's decision. It reveals both positive and negative news, data, financials, and operational details that impact its business.
-  The EGX develops and enforces the disclosure requirements that all listed companies must follow.
dividends-info:
• Cash : 
-  A cash dividend is a payment made by a company to its shareholders in the form of cash distributions as part of the company's current earnings or accumulated profits.
-   Dividend-paying companies are typically established, with stable cash flow, and beyond the growth stage.
• Stock:
-  A stock dividend is a payment to shareholders that consists of additional shares in the company rather than cash. 
-   The distributions are paid in fractions per existing share
-   A company may prefer to pay dividends in stock rather than cash to preserve its cash reserves.
EGX100 EWI:
-  The index tracks the performance of the top 100 companies in terms of liquidity and activity which includes the constituents of EGX30 and the constituents of EGX70 EWI.
-The index is designed to balance the impact of price changes among the constituents of the index as they will weigh 1.00% at each quarterly review.
-  The index is in line with the requirements of the market participants, especially mutual fund managers who have the desire to have a benchmark index that uses an equal weight method.
-  EGX100 EWI constituents are rebalanced four times every year. At the 1st of February and the 1st of August, comprehensive rebalances are undertaken to adjust constituents (added or deleted). At the 1st of May and the 1st of November, partial rebalances are undertaken to adjust only Adjusted Weight Factor of the constituents without any modification in the constituents.
EGX30:
-  EGX 30 index includes the top 30 companies in terms of liquidity and activity.
- EGX 30 Index is weighted by market capitalization and adjusted by the free float.
- EGX 30 constituents are reviewed on a semi-annual basis (1st of February and 1st of August).
-  The number of included companies from the same sector should not exceed five companies.
EGX30CAPPED:
-  This index is designed to capture the performance of the most active 30 companies in terms of traded value and liquidity, while capping the weight of any constituent to a maximum of 15%.
-The index sets a maximum percentage on the weight of the constituents that is determined by its adjusted market capitalization. Constituents are capped quarterly so that the weight of each constituent will not exceed 15% of the total weight. This would minimize the control of a limited number of constituents on the overall performance of the index.
-  Constituents of the index are rebalanced four times every year. At the 1st of February and the 1st of August, comprehensive rebalances are undertaken to adjust constituents (added or deleted). At the 1st of May and the 1st of November, partial rebalances are undertaken to adjust only Adjusted Weight Factor of the constituents without any modification in the constituents.
EGX50:
-  The index includes the top 50 companies in terms of liquidity and activity.
- The index is designed to balance the impact of price changes among the constituents of the index as they will have a fixed weight of 2% at each quarterly review.
-  EGX50 EWI constituents are rebalanced four times every year. At the 1st of February and the 1st of August, comprehensive rebalances are undertaken to adjust constituents (added or deleted). At the 1st of May and the 1st of November, partial rebalances are undertaken to adjust only Adjusted Weight Factor of the constituents without any modification in the constituents.
EGX70 EWI:
-  The Index includes the top 70 companies in terms of liquidity and activity after excluding the constituents of EGX30. EGX70 EWI avoids concentration on one industry, therefore has a good representation of various industries/sectors in the economy.
-  The index is designed to balance the impact of price changes among the constituents of the index as they will have a weight of 1.43% at each quarterly review.
-  EGX70 EWI constituents are rebalanced four times every year. At the 1st of February and the 1st of August, comprehensive rebalances are undertaken to adjust constituents (added or deleted). At the 1st of May and the 1st of November, partial rebalances are undertaken to adjust only Adjusted Weight Factor of the constituents without any modification in the constituents.

indices-list-info: Indices represent a basket of securities intended to replicate a certain area/sector of the market. An index is used as a method to track the performance of a group of Assets in a standardized way. Index performance is based on a certain calculated methodology.
internal-trades-info: Internal trades refer to when a company’s executives, board of directors, major shareholders, or affiliated groups buy or sell the company’s stock, hence it gets disclosed to the public by the exchange.

Special stocks - margin: The list of stocks eligible for margin trading mechanism (the purchase is financed by one of the financing entities). The percentages are announced by the Egyptian Exchange (EGX), valuing the shares in the customer's portfolio by 100% for the Category (A) list and by 80% for the Category (B) list. The EGX reviews the lists every six months (January and July).
Special stocks - short selling: The list of stocks eligible for short selling and it gets reviewed every six months by the stock exchange (January and July).
Special stocks – Same day: Referred to as T+0 is the list of stocks allowed to purchase and sale in the same trading session. Trading on these securities could be carried out more than once during the same trading session with a maximum limit of 1/10,000 of a company’s outstanding number of shares, provided the completion of selling the purchased number of shares in the same trading session.

TAMAYUZ:
-  This index comes within the EGX’s strategic framework and its interest to increase the liquidity of small- and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) aiming to achieve a promising Egyptian Capital Market.
-Tamayuz index targets companies that have strategic plans for expansion and continuous growth. The constituents of the index are selected using financial criteria to measure the companies’ level of profitability, which represented in positive operating cash flow from operating activities, positive growth rate for each of revenues and book value of the company.
-  Constituents of the index are weighted by free float-adjusted market capitalization and are subject to a 35% single constituent weight cap.
-  Tamayuz index companies are reviewed on semi-annual base (in April and October), whereby constituents are changed (added or deleted). At the 1st of August and the 1st of February, partial rebalances are undertaken to adjust only capping factors of the constituents without any modification in the constituents.
The maximum limit for one transaction on: The maximum limit for one transaction on InstaPay application is “EGP 70,000” , Please split the amount into two payments.

treasury-info:
-  Treasury stocks refer to previously issued outstanding shares that have been bought back by the issuing company, and they are not entitled to distributions or voting rights during its holding period. 
-  The reacquired shares are then held by the company for its own disposition. They can either remain in the company’s possession to be sold in the market, or the business can terminate the shares within maximum one financial year.

trading_terms_1: AF Securities, licensed by the Financial Regulatory Authority (License #453), provides investment services on the Egyptian Exchange (EGX). As the first online platform for executing transactions on the EGX, AF Securities aims to transform the dialogue surrounding Egypt's stock market, making it more inclusive and user-friendly. Our services are tailored to meet the needs of both novice traders and well-rounded investors.
